summary: Block-level GEMM: allocate A/B shared tiles and C fragment, clear accumulator, loop over K with T.Pipelined, copy global tiles to shared, call T.gemm, then copy fragment to global output.
control_flow_shape: 2-D grid over N/M tiles; K tile loop is pipelined; optional post-GEMM elementwise loop.
memory_flow_shape: Global A/B -> shared A/B -> fragment accumulator -> global C.
reuse_guidance: Start from this pattern for most dense GEMM variants; add epilogue in fragment before final T.copy.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.
limitations: Sparse/dequant/grouped variants add metadata/layout constraints that must be validated per device.

NVIDIA: cuda -arch=sm_80 for Ampere GEMM
cuda -arch=sm_90 for Hopper WGMMA/TMA-capable work
cuda -arch=sm_100a for Blackwell TCGEN05/TMEM work when supported
cutedsl for NVIDIA CuTe DSL backend when dependency is available
AMD: hip -mcpu=gfx90a/gfx940/gfx950; verify LDS and MFMA lowering
CPU: llvm or c; prefer CPU-specific usage and is_cpu=True rather than GPU shared-memory tuning
Apple/WebGPU: low confidence for tensor-core GEMM; use generic elementwise/copy patterns first

strategy_notes: Tune block_M/block_N/block_K/threads/num_stages against shared memory/register limits.
Confirm dtype and accumulator support for target tensor-core path.
Avoid explicit WGMMA/TCGEN unless device architecture supports it.

summary: Adds a third grid dimension for split-K partitions; each CTA computes a partial K range and atomically accumulates partial results into C.
control_flow_shape: 3-D grid over N/M/split_k; inner K loop ranges over each split partition.
memory_flow_shape: Global A/B split tiles -> shared -> fragment -> shared/local partial -> atomic global accumulation.
reuse_guidance: Use when K is large enough to expose more CTAs; confirm atomic accumulation dtype and output initialization.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.
limitations: Sparse/dequant/grouped variants add metadata/layout constraints that must be validated per device.

summary: Sparse GEMM stages compressed A values plus metadata E and dense B, then calls T.gemm_sp or T.gemm_sp_v2.
control_flow_shape: K tile pipeline with extra metadata copy per K tile.
memory_flow_shape: Compressed global A and metadata -> shared sparse operands -> sparse tensor core op -> fragment/global output.
reuse_guidance: Prefer gemm_sp_v2 when custom metadata compression or faster compilation is needed; verify metadata layout and architecture constraints.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.
limitations: Sparse/dequant/grouped variants add metadata/layout constraints that must be validated per device.

summary: Tiled QK then PV attention: stage Q/K/V tiles, compute scores with T.gemm, maintain row-wise max/sum statistics, apply exp/scale/mask, and write output without materializing full attention matrix.
control_flow_shape: 2-D or 3-D grid over query tiles, heads, batches; inner loop iterates key/value blocks with online softmax state.
memory_flow_shape: Global Q/K/V -> shared tiles -> fragment score/accumulators -> global output/statistics.
reuse_guidance: Reuse for attention kernels where memory traffic dominates; preserve numerical online-softmax update order and layout annotations.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.
limitations: Paged/varlen metadata and specialized barriers are easy to make device-specific.

NVIDIA: cuda -arch=sm_80+ for tensor-core QK/PV
cuda -arch=sm_90 for Hopper WGMMA/TMA-oriented kernels
cuda -arch=sm_100a for Blackwell-specific variants only with matching examples/tests
AMD: hip with exact gfx target; prefer AMD examples when available
CPU/Metal/WebGPU: low confidence for advanced flash/decode attention

strategy_notes: Choose block_M/block_N/head_dim tiling based on shared memory and register pressure.
Preserve online softmax order across devices.
Target-specific layout annotations may be required for QK/PV GEMMs.

summary: Decode attention examples organize KV cache pages or blocks, compute QK scores, reduce/select over sequence blocks, then compute weighted V output.
control_flow_shape: Grid maps heads/batches/pages; loops traverse cache blocks and reduce over active tokens.
memory_flow_shape: Paged global cache -> shared/fragments -> score/reduction fragments -> output.
reuse_guidance: Use when operator needs KV-cache indexing; double-check page metadata, variable sequence lengths, and output layout.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.
limitations: Paged/varlen metadata and specialized barriers are easy to make device-specific.

NVIDIA: cuda -arch=sm_80+ for tensor-core QK/PV
cuda -arch=sm_90 for Hopper WGMMA/TMA-oriented kernels
cuda -arch=sm_100a for Blackwell-specific variants only with matching examples/tests
AMD: hip with exact gfx target; prefer AMD examples when available
CPU/Metal/WebGPU: low confidence for advanced flash/decode attention

strategy_notes: Choose block_M/block_N/head_dim tiling based on shared memory and register pressure.
Preserve online softmax order across devices.
Target-specific layout annotations may be required for QK/PV GEMMs.

summary: Uses local/shared fragments and reductions to compute stable max/sum updates without materializing intermediate matrices.
control_flow_shape: Parallel tile loop with reduction along last or selected dimension; may keep running max/sum state.
memory_flow_shape: Global/shared input -> fragment reductions -> global/stat output.
reuse_guidance: Use for softmax, norm, top-k preselection, and attention statistics; output shape must match reduce dim rules.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.

NVIDIA: cuda; use warp/block reductions where supported
AMD: hip; account for wave/LDS behavior
CPU: llvm/c; avoid GPU warp assumptions
Apple/WebGPU: prefer generic T.Parallel + reduce patterns and validate

strategy_notes: Tune reduction tile size to fit fragment/shared memory.
Check NaN behavior and warp reducer availability per backend.

summary: Convolution examples use TileLang tiling and may rely on c2d_im2col/copy staging to map convolution windows to GEMM-like computation.
control_flow_shape: Grid covers output channel/spatial tiles; inner loops traverse reduction/filter dimensions.
memory_flow_shape: Global activation/filter -> shared/im2col tile -> fragment accumulation -> global output.
reuse_guidance: Prefer if the convolution can be lowered to GEMM-like tiled accumulation; verify layout, padding, and boundary masks.

summary: Maps one or more tensors to block tiles, uses T.Parallel for per-element operations, optionally stages vectors into local buffers with T.copy.
control_flow_shape: 1-D or 2-D grid; T.Parallel maps tile elements inside each block.
memory_flow_shape: Global input -> direct per-element operation or local registers -> global output.
reuse_guidance: Use for simple fused epilogues or standalone elementwise operators; guard boundaries when total size is not divisible by tile size.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.

NVIDIA: cuda with coalesced T.Parallel/T.copy; add vectorization after validation
AMD: hip with LDS/register-aware tile sizes
CPU: llvm/c; simple loops may be preferable
Apple/WebGPU: more portable than tensor-core patterns, but validate codegen

strategy_notes: Start with generic T.Parallel and boundary guards.
Add coalesced_width/vectorization hints only after target selection.

summary: Top-k example combines per-block scanning, local/shared candidate storage, comparisons, and reductions to emit selected values/indices.
control_flow_shape: Grid over rows/batches; inner loops scan candidate blocks and maintain local top-k state.
memory_flow_shape: Global scores -> local/shared candidate buffers -> global values/indices.
reuse_guidance: Use as a reference for selection/fused routing kernels; check K bounds and index dtype.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.

NVIDIA: cuda with coalesced T.Parallel/T.copy; add vectorization after validation
AMD: hip with LDS/register-aware tile sizes
CPU: llvm/c; simple loops may be preferable
Apple/WebGPU: more portable than tensor-core patterns, but validate codegen

strategy_notes: Start with generic T.Parallel and boundary guards.
Add coalesced_width/vectorization hints only after target selection.

summary: Decorator-based autotuning enumerates configs for block sizes, thread count, and pipeline stages, then benchmarks candidate compiled kernels.
control_flow_shape: Host-side config loop around repeated kernel elaboration/compile/benchmark.
memory_flow_shape: No kernel memory flow; tunes parameters that influence inner kernel memory flow.
reuse_guidance: Use when schedule knobs are not obvious; keep config space small enough for compile time.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.

NVIDIA: cuda with exact sm for cost model and benchmark fidelity
AMD: hip with exact gfx target
CPU: llvm/c, but autotuning value may be lower
Apple/WebGPU: validate backend support first

strategy_notes: Include device model in config records; benchmark on the target device, not a proxy.
Use pass_configs only to diagnose or intentionally constrain lowering.

summary: Stages packed low-bit weights, dequantizes into fragment/local buffers, then feeds dequantized tiles into T.gemm with optional layout annotations and autotune.
typical_inputs: scale or metadata tensors where required
typical_outputs: C or transposed Ct output tensor
control_flow_shape: 2-D GEMM grid with K pipeline; inside each K stage packed B is copied, unpacked/dequantized in T.Parallel, then used by T.gemm.
memory_flow_shape: Global activation/packed weight -> shared packed tile -> fragment/local dequantized tile -> GEMM accumulator -> shared/global output.
reuse_guidance: Use for quantized matmul variants; keep unpack/dequant math close to the K pipeline and verify output orientation/transposition.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.
limitations: Sparse/dequant/grouped variants add metadata/layout constraints that must be validated per device.

summary: Maps padded group row blocks to CTAs, derives current group from batch offsets, performs per-group tiled GEMM, and guards rows beyond actual group size on writeback.
control_flow_shape: Grid x dimension enumerates padded M blocks across all groups; y dimension enumerates N tiles; host precomputes group offsets.
memory_flow_shape: Global per-group A/B tiles -> shared -> fragment accumulator -> guarded global C writeback.
reuse_guidance: Use for MoE/expert batched GEMM. Treat offset tensors as part of ABI and guard tail rows with actual_rows.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.

summary: Computes per-row square sum with T.reduce_sum, transforms to inverse RMS, then applies scale/normalization through T.Parallel and T.copy.
typical_inputs: block row and K tile sizes
control_flow_shape: Grid over row blocks; one or two passes over columns depending on full-row vs split-K implementation.
memory_flow_shape: Global row tile -> shared/fragment -> reduction fragment -> normalized shared/local -> global output.
reuse_guidance: Use for norm-like row reductions; split-K version reduces memory footprint when N is too large for one shared tile.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.

NVIDIA: cuda; use warp/block reductions where supported
AMD: hip; account for wave/LDS behavior
CPU: llvm/c; avoid GPU warp assumptions
Apple/WebGPU: prefer generic T.Parallel + reduce patterns and validate

strategy_notes: Tune reduction tile size to fit fragment/shared memory.
Check NaN behavior and warp reducer availability per backend.

summary: Uses T.dynamic symbols for M/N/K while keeping block sizes compile-time parameters, then supplies concrete tensors and dynamic values at runtime/profiling.
control_flow_shape: Same as tiled GEMM, but grid extents and tensor shapes use dynamic symbolic variables.
memory_flow_shape: Global dynamic-shaped A/B -> shared tiles -> fragment accumulator -> dynamic-shaped C.
reuse_guidance: Use when dimensions vary at runtime; keep tiling parameters static and pass representative input_tensors for profiler.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.

summary: Uses explicit async tensor-core APIs (`T.wgmma_gemm` or `T.tcgen05_gemm`) and explicit waits/barriers instead of relying on `T.gemm` implicit synchronization.
control_flow_shape: Kernel body issues async tensor-core op, then explicit wait/barrier before reading accumulator.
memory_flow_shape: Global -> shared operands -> async tensor-core accumulator -> explicit wait -> global output.
reuse_guidance: Use only when manually scheduling Hopper/Blackwell tensor-core operations; verify architecture and wait/barrier placement.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing intrinsic path, tile sizes, num_stages, memory scopes, and validation backend.
limitations: Sparse/dequant/grouped variants add metadata/layout constraints that must be validated per device.

strategy_notes: Tune block_M/block_N/block_K/threads/num_stages against shared memory/register limits.

pattern_name: SM100 TCGEN05 GEMM with TMA and warp specialization
summary: Uses Blackwell-specific TMEM, TMA copies, explicit mbarriers, optional 2CTA cluster launch, and warp-specialized producer/consumer structure for TCGEN05 GEMM.
control_flow_shape: One warp or CTA group issues TMA loads, another issues TCGEN05 GEMM, then waits before copying TMEM accumulator to output.
memory_flow_shape: Global A/B -> staged shared tiles via TMA -> TCGEN05 TMEM accumulator -> fragment/shared -> global C.
reuse_guidance: Use only for confirmed Blackwell/SM100 targets. Preserve barrier parity, TMEM lifetime, cluster_dims, and producer/consumer handoff from the closest official example.

device_strategy.target_selection: Require an exact Blackwell target such as cuda -arch=sm_100a before reusing this pattern.
limitations: Preview SM100 support; barrier protocol and pass configs must be validated on target hardware.
Not portable to Ampere, generic CUDA, HIP, CPU, Metal, or WebGPU.

NVIDIA: cuda -arch=sm_100a only when the local TileLang checkout and hardware support TCGEN05/TMEM
AMD: not applicable
CPU: not applicable
Apple/WebGPU: not applicable

strategy_notes: Confirm T.tcgen05_gemm signature with lookup_apis.
Inspect examples/gemm_sm100 source before editing barrier counts or cluster_dims.
Prefer T.gemm with mbar only when the synchronous path is sufficient.

summary: Distributes K-iteration tiles across SM-count-sized programs, atomically accumulates partial tiles, and then computes full tiles to improve occupancy for irregular GEMM shapes.
control_flow_shape: Single grid over program IDs; each program handles a Stream-K iteration interval first, then full tiles assigned by SM partitioning.
memory_flow_shape: Global A/B tiles -> shared -> fragment accumulator -> direct or atomic global C writeback.
reuse_guidance: Use for GEMM shapes where standard tile-per-CTA scheduling underutilizes SMs. Validate atomic accumulation dtype and tile ownership carefully.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target and SM count before choosing streamk_programs and partition factors.
limitations: Example is CUDA-oriented and assumes explicit total_sm and atomic accumulation choices.

NVIDIA: cuda with known SM count; validate atomics and profiler backend
AMD: possible only after adapting wave/LDS/atomic behavior and validating HIP examples
CPU: not applicable
Apple/WebGPU: not applicable

strategy_notes: Do not reuse total_sm constants blindly.
Keep output initialization and partial-tile atomic accumulation explicit.
Compare against standard tiled GEMM before keeping Stream-K complexity.

pattern_name: Fused MoE GEMM with shared and routed experts
summary: Fuses expert gate/up/down GEMM stages with SiLU-style activation and grouped routed-expert indexing for MoE inference/training kernels.
control_flow_shape: Multiple GEMM-like kernel regions compute shared experts, routed gate/up projections, activation/product, and down projection with group-offset guarded rows.
memory_flow_shape: Token/weight tiles -> shared/fragments -> GEMM accumulators -> fused activation/intermediate -> down GEMM -> output.
reuse_guidance: Use for MoE kernels only after confirming ABI tensors for group sizes, offsets, padded offsets, expert weights, and output layout.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before tuning expert tile sizes, pass configs, and profiler backend.
limitations: MoE ABI and expert routing metadata are application-specific; validate against the local model runtime.

NVIDIA: cuda; confirm warp-specialization pass configs and tensor-core dtype support
AMD: hip only after adapting grouped GEMM and activation behavior
CPU: not a performance target for this pattern
Apple/WebGPU: low confidence

strategy_notes: Preserve grouped offset guards for actual_rows.
Check activation numerical behavior against PyTorch reference.
Treat pass_configs as part of the pattern, not incidental boilerplate.

pattern_name: Low-precision FP8/INT4 GEMM and casting examples
summary: Indexes official FP8, INT4, per-token cast, and DeepGEMM examples for low-precision GEMM workflows that combine quantization, casting, and tensor-core GEMM.
control_flow_shape: GEMM-like tiled kernels with pre/post cast or dequant steps near the K pipeline or token dimension.
memory_flow_shape: Global low-precision or packed tensors -> shared/local decode or cast -> GEMM accumulator -> output/reference comparison.
reuse_guidance: Use as a source index for FP8/INT4 work. Confirm exact dtype, scale layout, packing format, and target tensor-core support before adapting.

device_strategy.target_selection: Require exact device and dtype support before choosing FP8/INT4 lowering.
limitations: Low-precision layouts and scale tensors are example-specific.
SM100 FP8 paths are not portable to older architectures without validation.

NVIDIA: cuda with architecture-specific FP8/INT4 support confirmed by source examples
AMD: hip only using AMD FP8 examples and exact gfx target
CPU: not a performance target
Apple/WebGPU: low confidence

strategy_notes: Check scale tensor ABI and output dtype first.
Do not mix FP8 examples across CUDA architectures without source validation.

task_family: block-sparse GEMM, sparse tensor core, sparse attention
summary: Indexes official block-sparse GEMM, sparse tensor-core GEMM, block-sparse attention, and sparse decode examples.
control_flow_shape: Sparse metadata selects active blocks or compressed operands before tiled GEMM/attention inner loops.
memory_flow_shape: Sparse metadata + global operands -> selected shared tiles -> tensor-core or SIMT computation -> global output.
reuse_guidance: Use as a source index for sparse work. Treat metadata layout and active-block selection as part of the operator ABI.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before selecting sparse tensor-core or SIMT sparse path.
limitations: Sparse metadata formats vary by example and model runtime.

NVIDIA: cuda; verify sparse tensor-core and block metadata support
AMD: hip only after adapting metadata and MFMA/LDS behavior
CPU: not a performance target
Apple/WebGPU: low confidence

strategy_notes: Start from matching metadata ABI, not just operator name.
Validate dense reference comparison before benchmarking.

pattern_name: DeepSeek sparse MLA/NSA and attention sink examples
task_family: DeepSeek MLA/NSA/MHC and attention sink kernels
summary: Indexes DeepSeek MLA/NSA/V3.2, MHC, DSA sparse finetune, and attention sink examples for model-specific attention kernels.
control_flow_shape: Model-specific attention kernels combine tiled QK/PV or sparse MLA loops with metadata-indexed row/block selection.
memory_flow_shape: Attention operands and metadata -> shared/fragments -> score/reduction/selection state -> output tensors.
reuse_guidance: Use only after identifying the exact model ABI. Prefer nearest official example and preserve metadata semantics.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve CUDA/HIP target and model runtime constraints before adapting.
limitations: Highly model-specific; low portability outside the matching DeepSeek-style runtime.

NVIDIA: cuda; validate against provided reference/test scripts
AMD: hip only where AMD examples or adaptations exist
CPU: not a performance target
Apple/WebGPU: low confidence

strategy_notes: Treat metadata tensors and sequence layout as ABI.
Do not merge with generic flash attention without source comparison.

pattern_name: Linear attention, KDA, GDN, and state-space style examples
summary: Indexes official linear attention, Mamba-style chunk scan/state, KDA, and GDN examples.
control_flow_shape: Chunked sequence kernels mix tiled matrix operations, reductions, scans, and recurrence-style state updates.
memory_flow_shape: Global sequence/state tensors -> shared/fragments -> reduction or scan state -> output/gradient tensors.
reuse_guidance: Use as a source index for non-softmax attention/state-space operators. Confirm recurrence order and numerical stability against reference code.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before choosing chunk size and reduction strategy.
limitations: These examples are algorithmically diverse; inspect source before reusing control flow.

NVIDIA: cuda; validate chunk/reduction behavior
AMD: hip after adapting LDS/wave behavior
CPU: possible for correctness experiments, not performance
Apple/WebGPU: low confidence

strategy_notes: Preserve recurrence/scan ordering.
Use reference tests before tuning.

pattern_name: Vector transforms, GEMV, random, and layout-analysis examples
task_family: GEMV, Hadamard, random, cast, analysis, and layout helper kernels
summary: Indexes official GEMV, Hadamard transform, random, cast, analyze, plot_layout, and visual layout inference examples that are useful as supporting operator patterns.
control_flow_shape: Mostly one-dimensional or layout-oriented kernels with simple tiled/vector loops or analysis-only host workflows.
memory_flow_shape: Global/vector inputs -> local/shared fragments or analysis utilities -> output tensors or diagnostics.
reuse_guidance: Use as supporting source evidence for vector, cast, random, and layout questions rather than as the primary path for GEMM/attention generation.

device_strategy.target_selection: Resolve target before assuming random, layout, or vector lowering support.
limitations: Some source files are analysis/visualization workflows rather than deployable kernels.

NVIDIA: cuda for kernel examples; analysis scripts may be host-side
AMD: hip after checking source compatibility
CPU: possible for analysis or simple kernels
Apple/WebGPU: low confidence

strategy_notes: Distinguish deployable kernel examples from analysis utilities.
Use layout examples to inspect, not to infer performance claims.

pattern_name: Warp specialization, AutoDD, eager JIT, and model integration examples
task_family: advanced scheduling, debugging, eager JIT, and integration examples
summary: Indexes warp-specialized GEMM/FlashMLA examples, AutoDD bug-minimization examples, eager JIT notebooks, and BitNet integration kernels.
control_flow_shape: Advanced scheduling examples split producer/consumer work across warps or demonstrate debugging/integration workflows around TileLang kernels.
memory_flow_shape: Depends on selected example; generally staged global/shared operands with explicit barriers or model-specific packed weight flow.
reuse_guidance: Use only after reading the exact source. These examples are advanced scheduling/debug/integration references, not generic templates.

device_strategy.target_selection: Require exact target and pass configuration before reusing warp-specialized schedules.
limitations: Debug and notebook files are not always deployable operator templates.
BitNet integration depends on external model runtime assumptions.

NVIDIA: cuda; verify warp-specialization pass behavior and barrier protocol
AMD: low confidence unless adapted
CPU: not applicable for warp-specialized schedules
Apple/WebGPU: not applicable

strategy_notes: Treat pass configs and barrier placement as semantic requirements.
Do not copy integration kernels without their runtime ABI.
